response: In C# you can measure the time it takes to send a UDP packet by using the Stopwatch class. This will give you the duration from when the packet is sent until it is transmitted from the local machine. Note that this does not include the time it takes for the packet to reach the server. To do this you can start the Stopwatch before sending the packet and stop it right after the send operation completes. This will give you the time taken for the packet to be sent out. You can then output this time in milliseconds or another unit as needed. This method only measures the local transmission time and not the round trip or server response time.
